recalls.Description:
NICREW is recalling its Submersible RGB LED Lights, model number N21743, due to a serious ingestion hazard associated with the remote control's lithium coin battery. These lights, sold in a 15-inch black tube, come with a remote that features 21 buttons and a CR2032 battery. The design allows easy access to the battery, posing a risk to children, which violates federal safety regulations for consumer products containing coin batteries. Ingesting these batteries can lead to severe injuries or even death.

Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled lights immediately. It is crucial to remove the battery from the remote control and store it out of children's reach. NICREW is offering a free replacement remote and guidance on safe disposal of the product. To receive a replacement, customers must email their Amazon order number along with a photo showing the destroyed remote to nicrew@support.com. Both NICREW and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers directly regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: NICREW Submersible RGB LED Lights Recalled Due to Ingestion Risk and Regulatory Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Nicrew at nicrew@support.com or visit www.nicrew.com/n21743-recall/ for more details.

Products.Name: NICREW 15" Submersible RGB LED Lights with Remote

Images.Caption: Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control
Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control packaging

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Amazon.com from May 2024 to August 2024 for approximately $17.

Distributors.Name: Nicrew Technology Co. Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard from accessible lithium coin battery in remote control

Remedies.Name: Free replacement remote and disposal instructions

recalls.metaDescription: NICREW recalls Submersible RGB LED Lights due to ingestion risk from accessible coin batteries. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: NPW Group has issued a recall for their Halloween Projector Flashlights due to potential fire and burn hazards. These orange flashlights, featuring white stripes and a black end piece, measure approximately six inches in length and one inch in width. Each flashlight includes three interchangeable Halloween-themed projector covers displaying pumpkin, ghost, and skull designs, which can be fitted onto the black end piece. The device operates on two AA batteries.
recalls.Title: NPW Group Recalls Halloween Projector Flashlights Due to Fire and Burn Risks; Available Exclusively at Target

Injuries.Name: The firm has received nine reports of overheating including six reports of smoke or fire, resulting in four reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target stores nationwide and online at www.target.com in September 2024 for approximately $3.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating and melting casing

Remedies.Name: Return to Target for a full refund

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled flashlights immediately and return them to any Target store for a full refund. It's advised to turn off the flashlight, remove the batteries, and label the product as 'recalled' with a permanent marker before returning it. Target is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.
recalls.metaDescription: NPW Group recalls Halloween Projector Flashlights due to fire and burn hazards. Return to Target for a full refund.
